logic: Project land must meet forest definition threshold for 10+ years. Mangroves exempt from height.
notes: Document host-country forest definition and 10-year evidence.
source_span_text: Land in the project area has qualified as forest (following the definition used by the VCS; in addition, see Section 5.1.2) for at least the 10 years prior to the project start date.

conditions: Project area documented as meeting host-country forest definition for 10+ years prior to project start
exceptions: Cannot demonstrate 10-year forest qualification or forest definition not met

logic: Binary classification: unplanned (no legal right) vs planned (legally permitted conversion). Determines which BL module applies.
notes: Provide evidence of legal status of deforestation agents.
source_span_text: Baseline deforestation in the project area falls within either of the following categories: (a) Unplanned deforestation (VCS category AUDef) (b) Planned deforestation (VCS category APDef)

conditions: Baseline deforestation categorized as AUDef or APDef with supporting evidence
exceptions: Cannot determine deforestation category or deforestation does not fall into AUDef/APDef

logic: Three criteria must ALL be met. Excludes large-scale industrial agriculture/aquaculture.
notes: Document agent profiles and historical deforestation evidence.
source_span_text: Baseline agents of deforestation meet all of the following criteria: a) Clear the land for tree harvesting, settlements, crop production, ranching or aquaculture; b) Have no documented and uncontested legal right to deforest; c) Have caused deforestation in the historical reference period.

conditions: All three agent criteria demonstrated with verifiable evidence
exceptions: Any of the three criteria not met or insufficient evidence

logic: Must have legal documentation authorizing conversion.
notes: Provide legal permits and documentation.
source_span_text: Where conversion of forest lands to a deforested condition is legally authorized and documented.

conditions: Legal authorization for forest conversion documented and verified
exceptions: No legal authorization exists or authorization is contested

logic: Water table lowering is prohibited except for two specific exceptions.
notes: Monitor water table levels throughout crediting period.
source_span_text: Project activities lower the water table, unless the project converts open water to tidal wetlands, or improves the hydrological connection to impounded waters.

conditions: Water table not lowered, or documented exception (open water conversion/hydrological improvement)
exceptions: Water table lowered without qualifying exception

logic: Absolute prohibition on organic soil burning as a project activity.
source_span_text: Project activities include the burning of organic soil.

conditions: No organic soil burning documented in project area
exceptions: Organic soil burning occurs as project activity

logic: No nitrogen fertilizer application allowed during entire crediting period.
notes: Document land management practices to confirm compliance.
source_span_text: Nitrogen fertilizer(s), such as chemical fertilizer or manure, are applied in the project area during the project crediting period.

conditions: No nitrogen fertilizer application during crediting period
exceptions: Nitrogen fertilizers applied in project area

logic: Spillover emissions from hydrological changes must be assessed and not be significant.
notes: Use T-SIG to assess significance.
source_span_text: Hydrological connectivity of the project area with adjacent areas leads to a significant increase in GHG emissions outside the project area.

conditions: Hydrological connectivity assessed; no significant off-site GHG increase
exceptions: Significant GHG emissions increase outside project area due to hydrological changes

expected_evidence.label: Off Site Ghg Impact Analysis
expected_evidence.description: Required: off site ghg impact analysis

logic: Three alternative conditions. Must demonstrate one with verifiable evidence.
notes: Provide laws, management plans, annual reports, or market studies.
source_span_text: Prior to the project start date, the project area meets one of: (a) free of any land use that may be displaced; (b) under land use where displacement is prohibited by enforced law; (c) under land use that continues at similar or greater level.

conditions: One of three conditions (a, b, or c) demonstrated with verifiable evidence
exceptions: Cannot demonstrate any of the three conditions

logic: Must meet VCS peatland definition based on IPCC organic soil thresholds.
notes: Soil sampling required to confirm organic soil classification.
source_span_text: Rewetting drained peatland (RDP) activities occur on project areas that meet the VCS definition for peatland.

conditions: Soil sampling confirms organic soil meets IPCC Wetlands supplement thresholds
exceptions: Soil does not meet organic soil thresholds

logic: Must include at least one defined restoration activity type.
notes: Describe specific restoration actions in PD.
source_span_text: Project activities restoring tidal wetlands include any of: creating/restoring hydrology, altering sediment supply, changing salinity, managing vegetation, prescribed burning.

conditions: At least one defined restoration activity type documented and implemented

logic: Must include at least one defined conservation activity type.
source_span_text: Project activities conserving tidal wetlands include: protecting at-risk wetlands, improving water management, maintaining water quality, recharging sediment, creating accommodation space.

conditions: At least one defined conservation activity documented

logic: Parallel to AUDef criteria but for wetland degradation.
source_span_text: Baseline agents of wetland degradation meet all of: (a) cause alteration in hydrology or loss of SOC; (b) have no documented legal right; (c) have degraded wetlands in historical reference period.

exceptions: Any criteria not met or insufficient evidence

logic: IFM and ARR explicitly excluded. ARR in wetlands requires VM0047 + WRC modules.
notes: ARR in peatlands must include rewetting.
source_span_text: This methodology does not apply to ARR project activities. Where WRC projects using this methodology also implement ARR activities, projects must use an appropriate ARR methodology (e.g., VM0047).

conditions: IFM/ARR activities not claimed under this methodology; separate methodology used if applicable
exceptions: IFM or ARR activities claimed under VM0007

logic: Two specific leakage prevention activities are explicitly prohibited.
source_span_text: Leakage prevention activities include: (a) Flooding agricultural lands to increase production; and/or (b) Intensifying livestock production through use of feed-lots and/or manure lagoons.

conditions: Leakage prevention does not include prohibited activities

logic: Six required data elements for each discrete land parcel.
notes: Boundaries are fixed ex-ante and cannot change.

logic: Strict no-overlap rule. REDD+WRC combination is the sole exception.

summary: Exclusion of land in other GHG programs
logic: Double-counting prevention. Must track and report exclusions.
notes: Cross-reference with other registry records.

logic: RRD required for baseline modeling. Must be in same jurisdiction with similar characteristics.
Proxy areas required for planned deforestation baseline.
Stratification mandatory. Different activity types must be in separate strata.

summary: Carbon pool selection per activity type
logic: Pool inclusion/exclusion must be justified. If included in baseline, must also be in project and leakage.
notes: Selection documented in PD and monitoring report.

logic: AGB always mandatory. HWP and dead wood conditionally mandatory based on baseline/project comparison.
WRC focuses on SOC pool. Biomass pools excluded unless ARR applies.
GHG sources must be identified per Table 7 with inclusion/exclusion justification.
notes: Use T-SIG to test significance of excluded sources.

logic: GHG sources per Tables 8-9. Mandatory for CO2 from SOC, CH4 from SOC, combustion gases.
Symmetric accounting: baseline inclusion implies project inclusion.
Fixed period for baseline modeling. Duration per VCS Standard.
20-100 year range. Must be specified in PD.
100-year SOC horizon test. Must show significant difference. Assessed ex-ante conservatively.
notes: Procedures in Module X-STR.

logic: Sea-level rise must be factored into boundary delineation for tidal projects.
VT0001 mandatory. Stepwise approach: list alternatives, barrier/investment analysis, select most plausible.
Three mandatory alternatives unless demonstrated not credible or non-compliant.
Two-step: barrier analysis first, then investment analysis or qualitative GHG estimation as fallback.
Three options with different selection criteria. Option I is cost-only, Option II is IRR/NPV, Option III is benchmark-based.
Module selection based on deforestation category.
Module selection depends on substrate (peat vs tidal) and activity type (CIW vs RWE vs combined).
Periodic reassessment per VCS Standard. Must capture changes in deforestation drivers/behavior.
notes: Reassessments must capture changes in drivers and behavior.

logic: JNR data is acceptable as a conservative source even if less accurate.
notes: Must demonstrate JNR compliance.

logic: VT0001 mandatory for all non-tidal-wetland activities.
Activity method (ADD-AM) replaces project method (VT0001) for tidal wetlands only.
Standard REDD equation. Three components summed over monitoring period.
notes: Use Equations 2, 3, 4 for component breakdown.

logic: Parallel to REDD equation. Components use peat/tidal wetland modules.
Three leakage components. Activity shifting via LK-ASP and LK-ASU modules. Market effects via LK-ME.
Three components including ecological leakage specific to wetlands.
Three buffer components. Each = (baseline - project) × Buffer%. Buffer% from T-BAR.
notes: Buffer% varies by risk classification.

logic: 15% threshold at 90% CI. Excess deducted proportionally. Below threshold, no deduction.
Period credits = incremental adjusted NER minus buffer withholding.
Five carbon pool modules. Each optional except AG biomass per R-2-0008.
Module selection by activity type and emission source.
Four mandatory monitoring tasks. Each requires technical description, data list, procedures, QA/QC, archiving, responsibilities.
Six required content elements per task.
GPS or georeferenced data mandatory. Strata included.
Six minimum SOP/QA/QC elements.
notes: Adapt from national land use monitoring SOPs where available.

logic: 10-year minimum frequency. Spatial variables must be refreshed.
Three ongoing compliance checks. Fire triggers premium cancellation.
notes: Water depth measured before each verification per M-PEAT or M-TW.

logic: IPCC 2006 expert judgment protocol mandatory. Rationale must be documented.
Three data source tiers: literature > IPCC defaults > expert opinion.
notes: If uncertainty is significant, choose data that underestimates benefits.
